key_themes: Strong Q1 revenue growth and earnings beat with raised full-year guidance
Segment recovery in construction, forestry, and small agriculture
Long-term pivot to precision agriculture, automation, and software revenue
Broad analyst support with Buy ratings and 10-20% upside in price targets
Ongoing cyclical pressures, tariffs impacting margins, and valuation concerns
summary: Deere & Company (DE) reported Q1 FY2026 net sales of $9.61B (up 13% YoY) with EPS of $2.42 that significantly beat expectations, driving raised full-year net income guidance to $4.5-5.0B despite a 25% YoY drop in net income from tariffs, mix shifts, and warranty costs.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/deere-co-q1-2026-earnings-210439899.html)[[2]](https://s22.q4cdn.com/253594569/files/doc_financials/2026/q1/DE-1Q26-News-Release.pdf) Strength in construction/forestry (+34% sales) and small ag/turf (+24%) offset softer large ag results, with management calling 2026 the cycle bottom while emphasizing precision ag innovation and software monetization.[[3]](https://www.kwqc.com/2026/02/19/deere-reports-mixed-q1-results-with-revenue-growth-lower-profits/) Analysts lean bullish (Moderate Buy consensus, PTs ~$550-687 implying upside from ~$590 levels), and the stock is a noted holding in Bill Gates' portfolio, though some investor commentary flags rich multiples amid mixed profit trends.[[4]](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stocks/189-year-old-dividend-stock-deere-offers-19-upside-in-march-2026)[[5]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/deere-top-stock-pick-bill-152348082.html)
sources: 20+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, company releases, analyst notes, Seeking Alpha), 30+ X posts (semantic/keyword searches showing moderate retail discussion volume)
